rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2016, in Department 503, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LEONARD DELOUIZ's Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. =(503/GLW)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2016, in Department 503, Line 2, DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Certainteed Corporation's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Assuming defendant sustained its initial burden, plaintiff presented evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Such evidence includes the testimony of Lloyd Ambler (Exhibits B, G, P, R and T) and Leon Horowitz (Exhibits H, I), Views and Arguments document (Exhibit C), defendant's responses to interrogatories (Exhibit A) as well as Exhibits K, L, N, Q and S. While plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. Still, the Court cannot conclude that no reasonable jury could find plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
